> 
> @brnyza You have to request `CAMERA` permission.
> Add
> 
> ```
> <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.CAMERA" />
> ```
> 
> to `android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml` and use some plugin to request permissions in app.
> 
> I use this plugin https://pub.dev/packages/permission_handler and then in app request permission with code like this:
> 
> ```dart
> PermissionStatus status = await Permission.camera.status;
> if (status.isUndetermined) {
>     await Permission.camera.request();
> }
> ```
> 
> I also fully uninstalled the app from phone and install it again.

@Paras-Gupta790 and all others, I have solved the file upload issue again with the following code. Tested on Galaxy S8 Android 9, release mode and debug mode with uploading website as https://pasteboard.co and https://dropzonejs.com, and `webview_flutter: ^1.0.7`. Tested both standard rendering method and new hybrid composition rendering method. Both images and file upload should function correctly now.

<details><summary>Instructions</summary>

1. Open `External Libraries > Flutter Plugins > webview_flutter > android > src.main> java.io. ...> FlutterWebView.java`.
2. Replace code in this file from the code [here](https://gist.github.com/tneotia/ccdb84ba66d6863980b9d558fb95312e).
3. Create a `newActivity.java` file in the same directory, copy the code from [here](https://gist.github.com/tneotia/adc61196cfa59a5a7bdef6efb0ab5883).

In your own app,
1. In `AndroidManifest`, add:
```xml
<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE"/>
<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE"/>
<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.CAMERA"/>
```
If you don't want the camera choosing capability you can leave out the camera permission.
2. Above the `.MainActivity` declaration in `AndroidManifest` add:
```xml
<activity
            android:name="io.flutter.plugins.webviewflutter.newActivity"
            android:theme="@android:style/Theme.Translucent.NoTitleBar"/>
```

The last thing left to do is in Dart. Use the `permission_handler` plugin (or something similar, for these instructions I am using that) to request for your permissions like so:
```dart
await Permission.camera.request();
await Permission.storage.request();
```
These requests return `bool`, so you can check whether the user granted or denied the permission and perform actions accordingly. Again, if you don't want the camera choosing functionality you don't need to request for the camera permission.

Depending on how the website is set up, you may need to use `JavascriptMode.unrestricted`:
```dart
WebView(
    initialUrl: your_url,
    javascriptMode: JavascriptMode.unrestricted,
    //any other parameters
),
```
</details>

Let's try to keep this thread clean and avoid quoting my huge post, this is why I put it in a dropdown - but I'm not sure if GitHub will preserve the dropdown when quoted. Let me know of any issues and I may be able to take care of them, and leave a thumbs up if it worked for you!

> await Permission.storage.request();
> These requests return bool, so you can check whether the user granted or denied the permission and perform actions accordingly. Again, if you don't want the camera choosing functionality you don't need to request for the camera permission.
>

One more thing - This will ask for user permissions at the beginning. Can we ask for user permissions whenever he tries to upload documents?

Correct me if i'm wrong, it seems impossible to keep asking for permission after user granted the permission once. On the other hand, if user permanently denied the permission request, there's no way we can request again but to prompt user to grant permission in app settings.

According to Google guidelines - **The app requests runtime permissions in context, when the functionality is requested, rather than upfront during app startup.**

We can check before asking permissions based on the status, which is either undetermined, granted, denied, restricted or permanentlyDenied. But IDk how to ask for permissions when the user tries to upload image (i.e. when the functionality is requested)

Yes, by applying the code changes from https://github.com/flutter/plugins/pull/3225 (Add file choose on Android), now the webview can handle file-input field correctly. I'm using webview_flutter: 2.0.0-nullsafety.3

However, please take note that the code changes will throw java.lang.IllegalArgumentException if you're using a plugin called "flutter_downloader". After removed flutter_downloader from pubspec.yaml, it is working flawlessly.

Thanks to the code contributor, @fesp
